title: Vizag-Style Compressor Hall Gas Leak + Hot-Work Fatality
location: Compressor Hall, Zone Alpha — Steel Plant (Simulated)
summary: Hydrogen sulphide accumulated in a confined compressor hall while a hot-work permit for welding was active on an adjacent flange. A welder ignited the gas; one fatality, two injuries. Gas sensor alarm had been silenced by the shift supervisor. Based loosely on publicly reported incidents at Indian steel facilities.
root_cause: Simultaneous hot-work and elevated gas concentration in a confined space with no active ventilation.
outcome: 1 fatality, 2 injuries. Permit system overhauled plant-wide.
source_note: Loosely based on public DGFASLI incident reports — names and exact locations anonymised.

title: Chlorine Release During Maintenance — Water Treatment Plant
summary: Maintenance team worked on a chlorine dosing pump without isolating the line. Gas detector showed 4 ppm (threshold: 1 ppm) but the alarm was attributed to residual odour. A fitting failure released a cloud; three operators required hospitalisation.
root_cause: Maintenance on live chemical line without gas isolation; alarm mis-attribution led to delayed response.
outcome: 3 hospitalised. Mandatory gas isolation procedure added to permit system.
source_note: Fictional — plausible scenario based on generic water-treatment facility hazard patterns.

title: Methane Pocket Ignition — Sewage Pumping Station
location: Confined Pump Chamber — Municipal Sewage Station
summary: A contractor entered a confined space wet well to inspect a pump. Gas meter was not calibrated; methane was at 18% LEL. A spark from the contractor's uncertified torch ignited the gas. The contractor escaped with burns. No hot-work permit had been issued.
root_cause: Uncalibrated gas detector, hot-work without a permit, and inadequate confined-space entry procedure.
outcome: 1 serious injury. Contractor company banned; entry procedure rewritten.
source_note: Fictional — represents common permit-bypass failure mode documented in literature.

title: Ammonia Refrigerant Leak During Routine Valve Maintenance
location: Cold Storage Compressor Room — Food Processing Plant
summary: During routine valve packing maintenance on an ammonia refrigeration line, a gasket failed. Gas levels reached 200 ppm (IDLH: 300 ppm). Two maintenance workers in the room were evacuated; one suffered respiratory irritation. Ventilation was offline for the maintenance window.
root_cause: Gasket failure during maintenance with ventilation offline and no stand-by gas monitoring.
outcome: 1 injury (minor). Mandatory portable gas monitoring during all refrigerant maintenance.
source_note: Fictional — based on ammonia refrigeration hazard patterns documented by HSE UK.

title: Hydrogen Purge Line Flash-Back — Petroleum Refinery
location: Hydrogen Unit, Purge Station — Petroleum Refinery
summary: A hot-work permit was issued for cutting a non-operational purge line. The line had not been properly blinded; residual hydrogen flowed from an adjacent operational unit. Ignition caused a flash-back. Two contract workers sustained burns.
root_cause: Inadequate line isolation before hot-work; residual flammable gas not confirmed absent.
outcome: 2 injuries (burns). Blind-flange confirmation added to hot-work permit checklist.
source_note: Loosely based on petroleum refinery near-miss patterns in PNGRB safety advisories — details anonymised.

title: Confined Space Asphyxiation — Fertiliser Plant Silo
summary: Three workers entered a urea storage silo to clear a blockage. Oxygen displacement by nitrogen blanketing had not been verified. Two workers became unconscious due to oxygen deficiency (O2 at 8%). One worker managed to raise the alarm. All three were rescued.
root_cause: Entry into nitrogen-blanketed silo without oxygen measurement; no attendant stationed outside.
outcome: 2 unconscious (recovered). Confined space entry procedure completely rewritten.
source_note: Fictional — represents the oxygen-deficiency failure mode documented in OISD guidelines.

title: Multi-Factor Blast Furnace Gas Escape — Steel Plant
location: Blast Furnace Gas Recovery Unit — Integrated Steel Plant
summary: Blast furnace gas (CO-rich) accumulated in a low-lying gas recovery area. Simultaneously: a hot-work permit for pipe repair was active, and a confined space entry team was inside an adjacent tap hole inspection pit. All three risk factors compounded into a near-miss evacuation. No ignition occurred, but the CO level reached 2,800 ppm (IDLH: 1,200 ppm).
root_cause: Concurrent hot-work, confined-space entry, and maintenance in a high-CO area without active gas monitoring across all three activities.
outcome: Full plant evacuation, no fatalities. Compound permit review system introduced.
source_note: Loosely based on Steel Authority of India plant incident patterns — details and location anonymised.

title: Hot-Work Permit Lapse — LPG Bottling Plant Fire
location: LPG Cylinder Filling Station — Gas Bottling Plant
summary: A welding crew's hot-work permit expired at 14:00 but work continued until 14:45. During this window, an LPG hose coupling failed and released vapour; the ongoing welding arc ignited it. A flash fire injured four workers before the gas supply was isolated.
root_cause: Hot-work continued after permit expiry; no supervisor audit of work cessation.
outcome: 4 injuries. Automated permit expiry alerts introduced.
source_note: Fictional — represents LPG facility permit-expiry failure mode.

title: Maintenance Shift Handover Gap — Chemical Reactor Overpressure
summary: During a maintenance shift handover, a partially open isolation valve was missed. Gas build-up in the reactor reached 140% of design pressure. The pressure relief valve opened, releasing chlorinated solvent vapour into the bay. No confined space entries were in progress but two maintenance workers were in the blast radius.
root_cause: Incomplete handover documentation; valve state not communicated across shift change.
outcome: 2 minor injuries. Digital handover system introduced with mandatory valve-state checklist.
source_note: Fictional — represents chemical plant shift-handover failure mode.

title: Manhole Entry Death — Petrochemical Tank Farm
location: Tank Farm Drain Sump — Petrochemical Complex
summary: A junior operator was instructed to enter a drain sump to retrieve a dropped tool. No confined space entry permit was raised; no gas test was conducted. H2S accumulation (380 ppm, immediately dangerous) rendered the operator unconscious within 90 seconds. A second worker attempting rescue also collapsed. Both were recovered by fire brigade.
root_cause: Confined space entry without a permit, no atmospheric test, and no standby attendant.
outcome: 2 hospitalised (recovered). Fatal near-miss led to mandatory entry permit for all sub-grade spaces.
source_note: Loosely based on public petrochemical incident records in the OISD database — details anonymised.

title: Compressor Seal Failure During Planned Maintenance Window
location: Compressor Hall — Natural Gas Processing Plant
summary: During a scheduled compressor overhaul, a replacement seal was incorrectly installed. On restart, the seal leaked natural gas; concentration reached 35% LEL before detection. A maintenance team of four was still in the hall performing punch-list items.
root_cause: Incorrect seal installation with personnel still in the hazard zone during restart sequence.
outcome: Immediate evacuation; no injuries. Restart procedure now requires zone clearance before energisation.
source_note: Fictional — represents compressor hall restart failure mode.

title: Simultaneous Permit Conflict — Pipeline Pig Trap
location: Pig Launcher Station — Natural Gas Pipeline
summary: Two separate work orders were issued simultaneously: one for hot-work on a bypass valve flange, and another for a confined-space entry into the pig trap barrel for internal inspection. The permits were raised by different supervisors unaware of each other. Gas at 68% LEL was detected only after both crews had entered the work area.
root_cause: Simultaneous conflicting permits issued by isolated supervisors with no cross-check system.
outcome: Evacuation, no injuries. Centralised permit management system mandated.
source_note: Fictional — represents simultaneous-permit failure mode, a common compound-hazard scenario.

title: Night Shift Maintenance Near-Miss — Coke Oven Battery
location: Coke Oven Battery No. 3 — Integrated Steel Plant
summary: A night-shift maintenance crew worked on a coke oven door seal without notifying the control room. Coke oven gas (hydrogen + methane) migrated into the work area. A checker torch used by the crew was the ignition source for a small flash fire. The crew self-extinguished the fire; two workers had singed eyebrows, no serious injuries.
root_cause: Uncoordinated maintenance without control room notification; open flame used in a gas-prone area.
outcome: 2 minor injuries. Night-shift permit-to-work policy mandated with control room sign-off.
source_note: Fictional — represents coke oven battery maintenance near-miss pattern.

title: Toxic Gas Ingress — Underground Cable Duct Entry
location: HV Cable Duct — Industrial Power Distribution Station
summary: An electrical maintenance team entered an underground cable duct to trace a fault. The duct had accumulated hydrogen from a damaged lead-acid battery bank above it. No gas test was performed. One worker became dizzy and was assisted out; full atmospheric test revealed H2 at 22% LEL.
root_cause: Entry into a confined underground space without atmospheric testing; hydrogen source not identified.
outcome: 1 near-miss (dizziness). Atmospheric test made mandatory for all sub-grade electrical enclosures.
source_note: Fictional — represents battery hydrogen hazard in confined underground spaces.

title: All-Factor Compound Event — Refinery Turnaround Gone Wrong
location: Crude Distillation Unit — Petroleum Refinery Turnaround
summary: During a refinery turnaround shutdown, four simultaneous activities converged: hot-work on a heat exchanger, confined space entry into the column sump, maintenance on the overhead condensate line, and a gas cloud from an inadequately purged hydrocarbon circuit. All four risk factors were active concurrently. A hydrogen-rich cloud ignited from the hot-work. Major fire requiring 6-hour suppression.
root_cause: All four compound risk factors active simultaneously; turnaround coordination failure; inadequate purge verification.
outcome: 3 injuries (burns), significant asset damage. Multi-point simultaneous work prohibition introduced.
source_note: Loosely based on documented petroleum refinery turnaround incidents — details and location anonymised.
